item.title: Deutsche Boerse acquires a $200 mln stake in Kraken
item.description: German exchange operator ​Deutsche Boerse said ‌on Tuesday that it ​had acquired ​a 200 million dollar ⁠stake ​in US-based cryptocurrency ​exchange Kraken.

item.title: J&J's first-quarter profit beats estimates even as Stelara sales disappoint
item.description: Johnson & Johnson reported first-quarter earnings that beat ​Wall Street expectations on Tuesday and raised its full-year forecast, as strong demand for cancer ‌drug Darzalex and psoriasis treatment Tremfya more than offset a steep falloff in sales of its blockbuster autoimmune drug Stelara.

item.title: BlackRock quarterly profit rises on active ETFs, performance fees
item.description: BlackRock reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, as the asset ​manager saw strong inflows into its exchange traded funds and ‌earned more from performance fees, sending its shares up 2.8% in trading before the bell.

item.title: US truckers' diesel spend hits record high on Middle East conflict
item.description: The more than 3 million U.S. truckers who move goods for everything from grocery stores to factories to construction sites are grappling with the highest diesel prices in years, compounding pressure on the industry as Iran war-related ​oil price spikes also threaten to weaken freight demand.

item.title: US small business sentiment falls to 11-month low in March
item.description: U.S. small-business sentiment dropped to an 11-month low in March as a surge in oil prices because of the ​war in Iran overshadowed the benefits from low taxes, a ‌survey showed.

item.title: Fed nominee Warsh files financial disclosure in step towards confirmation
item.description: Kevin Warsh, the former Federal Reserve governor chosen ​by President Donald Trump to run the central ‌bank, has submitted financial disclosures that are required for his nomination to advance through the Senate, beginning with a ​yet-to-be-scheduled hearing.

item.title: Kremlin says internet curbs are temporary, will be lifted once security threat passes
item.description: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that internet restrictions, ​which it conceded had caused disruption to many Russians, ‌were needed for security reasons, but that they were temporary in nature and would be lifted once it was ​safe to do so.

item.title: HSBC's Europe and Americas banking head Keefe departs
item.description: HSBC 's head of banking in Europe and the Americas ​Gerry Keefe has resigned, becoming the ‌latest senior banker to depart since the bank's global overhaul launched in 2024.

item.title: Delta Air Lines scales back sustainable fuel and net-zero targets, Bloomberg News Reports
item.description: Delta Air Lines quietly removed two key environmental targets from its sustainability webpage late last week, Bloomberg ​News reported on Tuesday.

item.title: US stock futures climb as traders cling to de-escalation hopes, await earnings
item.description: U.S. stock index futures rose on Tuesday as traders held ​onto hopes of a de-escalation in the Middle East conflict, while awaiting March producer price data ‌and a fresh round of corporate earnings.

item.title: BMW deliveries slide in Q1 as China, US weakness outweighs Europe growth
item.description: BMW reported a 3.5% drop in ​global deliveries for ‌the first three months of 2026 on Tuesday, ​as weak demand ​in China and the ⁠U.S. weighed on ​the German premium carmaker's ​sales.

item.title: United, American Airlines climb after news of Kirby floating merger with Trump
item.description: United Airlines and American Airlines shares rose in premarket trading on Tuesday after United CEO ​Scott Kirby, sources said, pitched the potential for ‌a merger of the two carriers to U.S. President Donald Trump in February.

item.title: China Evergrande's billionaire boss falls from circles of power to fraud plea
item.description: Mingling with power brokers at centenary celebrations of the Chinese Communist Party on July 1, 2021, a beaming Hui Ka Yan betrayed no sign of the pressure mounting on his property company, China Evergrande Group, the world's most indebted.

item.title: Citigroup upgrades US equities as tech strength and earnings outlook soothe Mideast war woes
item.description: Citigroup has turned bullish on U.S. equities, joining a flurry of brokerages betting on resilient corporate earnings, attractive valuations following recent pullbacks, and ​the rising contribution of U.S. technology stocks to global earnings growth.

item.title: Nornickel seeks new market for palladium in EVs with catalyst for lithium‑sulphur batteries
item.description: Russia's Nornickel said on Tuesday it aims to complete the development of a palladium-based catalyst for lithium-sulphur (Li-S) batteries ​within three years, potentially creating a major new source of ‌demand for palladium in electric vehicles.

item.title: Seeking shelter from war and stagflation? Take a look at China's bond market
item.description: Investors are scooping up Chinese debt, which has stood tall in sliding bond markets globally, betting that China's low inflation and preparedness for an oil shock allow it to resist raising interest rates.

item.title: Under pressure: Tracking the pain in G7 government debt
item.description: The world's major economies have seen their debt levels surge in recent years, while ever-increasing spending demands -- from ageing populations to climate change and defence -- are adding to the pressure.

item.title: LVMH shares fall after Iran war hits first-quarter sales
item.description: Shares in French luxury goods ‌group LVMH fell as much as 3% on Tuesday after first-quarter sales were hit as the war in Iran dented spending by Middle Eastern shoppers, further delaying a long-awaited recovery for the luxury sector leader.

item.title: Australia Inc starts to feel Iran war fallout, raising stagflation risk
item.description: Australia's corporates are starting to count the cost of the war in the Middle East, ​with profit warnings from two top companies and a crash in business sentiment pointing to pain from rising ‌prices, raising the risk of stagflation.

item.title: Exclusive: Chinese chipmaker YMTC plans new factories amid heightened US-Sino trade tensions, sources say
item.description: Chinese chipmaker Yangtze Memory Technologies (YMTC) aims to build two more factories ​in addition to one that will be completed this year, which will more than double its production capacity when ‌all three are up and running, people familiar with the plans said.

item.title: Wegovy-maker Novo Nordisk partners with OpenAI to speed drug development
item.description: Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk , which has fallen behind Eli Lilly in the immensely lucrative weight-loss drug market, said on Tuesday it is partnering with OpenAI to deploy artificial intelligence across its business, from drug discovery to ​manufacturing and commercial operations.

item.title: From airlines to banks: Australian, New Zealand firms feel Gulf crisis heat
item.description: Companies in Australia and New Zealand are beginning to signal the financial strain from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, as higher fuel prices stoke inflation, dent business and consumer confidence, and weigh on corporate earnings.

item.title: Publicis posts 4.5% net revenue organic growth, maintains lead in US and Chinese markets
item.description: Publicis on Tuesday reported ‌first-quarter net revenue organic growth of 4.5%, meeting company-provided consensus and outpacing advertising industry peers, as AI and acquisitions drove gains in key markets, including the U.S. and China.

item.title: Italy's surprise rise in exports to US masks deep fragility to tariffs
item.description: Italy was the only major EU country whose exports to the United States grew last year despite Donald Trump's tariffs. The result was hailed by Rome's government, ​but a closer look at the data shows one-off factors at play and little underlying resilience.

item.title: After tackling Kering's debt problems, de Meo faces the Gucci challenge
item.description: Seven months into the job, Luca de Meo has bought himself time as CEO of Gucci owner Kering . This week he has to prove he ​knows how to use it.

item.title: Sazerac won’t easily gatecrash Jack Daniel's-maker's merger talks
item.description: Privately held bourbon maker Sazerac has emerged as an interloper in Pernod-Ricard's ​attempt to merge with Jack Daniel's owner Brown-Forman, according to a source familiar with the matter, even though analysts still think Pernod's portfolio ‌and global reach make the French spirits maker a better fit.

item.title: Morning Bid: Fingers crossed for a resolution
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: China Evergrande founder pleads guilty to fraud in Shenzhen court
item.description: The founder of China Evergrande Group, the world's ​most indebted property developer, pleaded guilty to charges ‌including misuse of funds, fundraising fraud and illegally taking public deposits, a court in China's southern city of Shenzhen said.

item.title: Korean AI chip startup DeepX prepares public share offering
item.description: South Korean AI chip ​startup DeepX said ‌on Tuesday it is preparing to ​list its shares ​domestically, adding the firm ⁠is open ​to a possible U.S. ​listing after that.

item.title: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation faces investor scrutiny amid strategy shift, FT reports
item.description: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation is under scrutiny from some of its own backers ​as the company shifts its focus to the enterprise ‌market to fend off competition from Anthropic, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday.
